page_content:
ENGL338 Special Topics in Literary and Cultural Studies (3 Credits) Explore new topic areas in English literary and cultural studies. Repeatable to: 12 credits if content differs.

ENGL344 The Novel in the 19th-Century World (3 Credits) Explore 19th-century English-language novels drawn from around the world: England, Australia, India, South Africa, and the United States. Readings may include canonical novels (Charlotte Bronte's Jane Eyre and George Eliot's Middlemarch) as well as lesser known but extraordinary novels by authors such as Bankimchandra Chattopadhyay and Frances Harper. Topics to be addressed include realism, romance, nationalism, race and racial identity, feminism, sexuality, emigration, empire, and the function of the novel itself.

ENGL345 Poetry, Lyric, and Song (3 Credits) Explore the forms, sounds, images, and experiences of poetry and song. How does poetic language grapple with human complexity and difference? What is the relationship between writing, orality, and music in the history of poetry? How do poets express and represent gender, sexuality, race, power, resistance, revolution, and cultural change? Reading and listening assignments might include balladry, sonnets, metaphysical poetry, free verse, hip-hop, popular music, and more.
